name: RPG-7 Rocket Launcher
prompt:
Vietnam War era Soviet RPG-7 (Ruchnoy Protivotankoviy Granatomyot) rocket-propelled grenade launcher. Primary NVA/VC anti-armor weapon.

Physical structure (~0.95m launcher + ~0.45m warhead):
- Launch tube: cylindrical tube ~0.04m diameter, ~0.95m long
  - Flared muzzle: trumpet-shaped flare at front end
  - Rear venturi: wider exhaust cone at rear end
  - Heat shield: wooden or bakelite heat shield wrapped around middle section
- Grip assembly: pistol grip below tube with trigger mechanism
  - Trigger: standard pistol-type
  - Trigger guard
- Optical sight: PGO-7 scope mounted on left side bracket (small cylinder)
- Rocket/warhead (loaded):
  - PG-7V warhead: distinctive bulbous/pear-shaped warhead at front
  - Booster section: cylindrical section between warhead and tube
  - Stabilizer fins: 4 spring-loaded fins at rear of rocket (folded along tube when loaded)

Colors: Dark olive tube (0x444a3a), wood heat shield (0x7B5B3A), dark warhead (0x4a4a40), darker metal (0x333333)
Orientation: On shoulder rest, warhead pointing +Z
Scale: Real-world meters (~1.4m total with warhead loaded)
